scenario_results.reason: The lane progressed a standard activation from approved intake through scheduling, on-site execution, and proof-of-performance closure only when agreement, ticketing, credentialing, and evidence records aligned.
Missing sponsor, venue, and system prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative activation handling.
The lane refused to mark work complete when photo recap, placement confirmation, and reconciliation artifacts were absent.
Conflicting CRM, ticketing, and redemption records were treated as a reconciliation exception and not forced into completion or refund action.
Out-of-policy refund, credit, and makegood requests were packaged for authorized owners rather than approved in-lane.
Counterfeit-access and unsafe-setup reports triggered safety and fraud escalation instead of sponsor-service improvisation.
A high-volume event-weekend queue produced a sponsor-specific execution summary that preserved deadlines, blockers, and owner traceability.
The lane preserved sponsorship and venue terminology such as benefit grid, load-in, credential manifest, and proof of performance rather than collapsing into generic marketing language.
Pricing and commercial-authority requests were rejected cleanly and routed to adjacent specialists instead of being over-resolved in the fulfillment lane.
Missing tenant authority context and unavailable approval matrices triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Conflicting public ticket-fee rules, venue guest policy, and sponsor contract terms were escalated for precedence resolution.
Tenant-specific museum-festival constraints were applied as stricter overlays while preserving the public baseline and explicit assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside sponsorship activation execution and does not claim pricing override, refund or makegood approval, contract renegotiation, safety signoff, fraud adjudication, or legal interpretation authority.
